name: Pocket Note Runner
description: Read the project's pocket-note.md note and carry out exactly what it asks, scoped to this codebase.
content:
There is a `pocket-note.md` file in this project. It is a clean, faithful record of something I said — a directive, an insight, a change, a move, some context, whatever it is. Read it and do what it says, here, in this project.

Treat the note as the instruction. I have already decided what I want; it is in the file. Your job is to carry it out in this codebase — not to reinterpret it, expand it into a larger plan, turn it into a research task, or hand me a meta-prompt. If the note describes one small change, make one small change. If it describes several, do those. Match the scope of what the note actually asks for.

The note was dictated, so a name or term in it may be the wrong word for THIS project — the transcription inferred something that does not match what this codebase actually calls a file, module, or concept. When that is unambiguous from the project itself, use the project's real name and note that you did. Do not otherwise change the note's wording or intent.

Before you make any change, read enough of this project to be sure you understand what the note is asking for in context. If anything in the note is ambiguous, underspecified, or conflicts with what you find in the project — or with another part of the note — stop and ask me. List exactly what is unclear and what you would do under each reading. A short question now is always better than a confident wrong change.

When you are done, tell me plainly what you changed and why, and anything in the note you deliberately did not act on.
